Fern Poltrtcnl PRACTICEs CoiraulsSIoN
428 J Street r Suite 620 r Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
August 17,2006
Sharon L. Burris, District Secretary
Contra Costa Water District
1331 Concord Avenue
P. O. Box 94524
Concord, CA 94524
RE: Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. A-06-077a
Dear Ms. Burris:
Enclosed please find a corrected version of our advice letter issued in File No.
A-06-077. This amended letter corrects a misunderstanding regarding Contra Costa
Water District's status as a multi-county agency rather than a county ageficy. Please
discard the letter entitled File No. A-06-077 and replace it with this letter.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

j 
'
' , ,.'i L, ps 1ys.----- l, u,. ,' li t t,.., i. ,'. r
Enclosure
AK:rd
cc: Robert B. Maddow
By: Adrianne Korchmaros { 's.;..'
Political Reform Consultant II
Corrected Letter 08/ I 7/06
14!6


Farn Polrrrcer PRACrrcEs CouvtlsSIoN
428 J Street r Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
August 17,2006
Sharon L. Burris, District Secretary
Contra Costa Water District
1331 Concord Avenue
P. O. Box 94524
Concord, CA 94524
RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-06-077a
Dear Ms. Burris:
This letter is in response to your request on behalf of the Contra Costa Water District
regarding the conflict-of-interest code provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1
This amended letter corrects afactual misunderstanding regarding Contra Costa Water
District's stafus as a multi-county agency rather than a county agency. You should disregard
the Buruis Advice Letter, Our File No. 4-06-077.
QUESTION
Must certain employees of Legg Mason Equity Funds file the statement of economic
interest (Form 700), whether because of section 87200 or the Contra Costa Water District's
conflict of interest code?
CONCLUSION
None of the employees of Legg Mason Equity Fund will be required to file Form 700
either as "other public officials who manage public investments" or "consultants" under the
Contra Costa Water District's conflict of interest code.
I Govemment Code sections 81000 - 9l}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the Califomia Code of Regulations.
@+l


File No. A-06-077a
Page No. 2
FACTS
The law firm of Bold, Polisner, Maddow, Nelson, & Judson serves as General
Counsel to the Contra Costa Water District ("CCWD'). In this capacity,the firm has
submitted further facts relevant to the Burris Advice Letter,No. t-bO-Zi'onyoorbehalf
The additional facts arethat CCWD, a county water district, organizedand existing
under the provisigl of the county water district law (Water Code section 30000), is an
independent special district whose territorial jurisdictional boundary includes property
located both in 99"t* Costa County and in Alameda County. The initial request did not
specifu thatthe district operates in two counties.
Pursuant to the provisions of the County'Water District Law (Water Code sections
30,000 et seq.), the CCWD has its own retirement system ('?lan') over which the CCWD,s
Board of Directors is ultimately responsible and into which CCwb and employees make
contributions. An appointed Retirement Board oversees its operations and u cbwp manager
serves as its individual trustee. Investment of the Plan's funds is the responsibility of the
Retirement Board, which obtains advice pursuant to contracts with retir6ment plan design
professionals and an actuary. Plan funds are in the custody of a corporate trustee (i.e., a
ba*) and ate distributed in accordance with directives from CCWD to the investment
management firms.
The investment management firms are recoflrmerded by the Retirement Board (with
the approval of the Board of Directors) and charged with the responsibility and authoriiy to
invest portions of the Plan's funds in various ur.itr (e.g., stock, fixed income assets, real
estate). Your specffic question regards the decision toinvest the plan,s funds in the Legg
Mason Equity Fund ("Fund').
The Fund is an open-end management investment company (commonlyreferred to as
a "mutual fund"), which is registered as such with the U.S. Seiuriiies and Exclange
Commission ("SEC') under the Investment Company Act of 1940. The Fund,s shares,
including those shares that are held by the Plan on U"t Uf of plan participants, are registered
with the SEC pursuant to the Securities Act of 1933 and are availible for sale to the public.
Sales of the Fund's shares to the public, including sales to the plan and plan participants, are
govemed by the Fund's prospectus.
The Fund is an investment pool in that the investonents of all Fund shareholders are
pooled and then used to invest in particular securities. All securities in which the Fund
invests are held by the Fund and no shareholder has any rights or ownership to any of the
individual securities.that the Fund purchases. Fund shareh-olders, including-th" plun o,
behalf of Plan participants, hold shares in^tfre Fund, the price of which is cilculated daily and
is based on the underlying investments of the Fund. Alishareholders are treated equally and
no shareholder has the ability to mandate which particular security a Fund should buy or
sell.


File No. A-06-077a
Page No. 3
All of the investment decisions for the Fund are made by arrinvestment manager that
the Fund has contracted with at armsJength. The investment decisions made on behalf of the
Fund are consistent with the Fund's investment objective and strategies as described in the
prospectus, and are for the benefit of all Fund shareholders.
Neither the investment manager for the Fund nor the Fund's principal
underwriter/distributor, Legg Mason Investor Services, LLC, has the authority or discretion
to authorize any buy or sell orders of Fund shares on behalf of the Plan or Plan participants.
ANALYSIS
Under the Act, state and local public officials file periodic statements of economic
interests (Form 700) for the purpose of disclosing pertinent economic interests. These
individuals are required to file the Form 700 pursuant to either section 87200 or section
87300. We will begin our analysis with section 87200, which imposes broad disclosure
obligations on various high level public officials, including "otherpublic officials who
managepublic investments." (Section87200.) Regulation 18701(b)(l) defines "otherpublic
officials who manage public investments" to include:
"(A) Members ofboards and commissions, includingpension and
retirement boards or commissions, or of committees thereof, who exercise
responsibility for the management of public investments;
(B) High-level officers and employees ofpublic agencies who exercise
pimary responsibility for the management of public investments, such as
chief or principal investment officers or chief financial managers. This
category shall not include officers and employees who work under the
supervision of the chief or principal investment officers or the chief financial
managers; and
(C) Individuals who, pursuant to a contract with a state or local
government agency, perform the same or substantially all the same functions
that would otherwise be performed by the public officials described in
subdivision(bx 1 XB) above. "
Regulation 18701(b) (2), (3) and (4) set forth the following definitions:
"(2) 'Public investments' means the investment of public moneys in
real estate, securities, or other economic interests for the production of
revenue or other financial refurn.
(3) 'Public moneys' means all moneys belonging to, received by, or
held by, the state, or any city, county, town, district, or public agency therein,
or by an officer thereof acting in his or her official capacity, and includes the
proceeds of all bonds and other evidences of indebtedness, trust funds held by
public pension and retirement systems, deferred compensation funds held for


File No. A-06-077a
Page No. 4
investments by public agencies, and public moneys held by a financial
institution under a trust indenture to which a public agency is a party.
(4)' Management of public investments' means the following non-
ministerial functions: directing the investment of public moneys; formulating
or approving investment policies; approving or establishing guidelines for
asset allocations; or approving investment transactions.,,
Although managers of the Fund exercise discretion and make investment decisions
when they invest the money CCWD invests in the mutual fund, the Commission has
interpreted the phrase "other public officials who manage public investments" to not include
mutual fund managers merely because a public agency chooses to invest its public moneys in
the mutual fund just as any member of the public. (Tolentino Advice Lettei,No. t-9+-:i9.)
Therefore, the managers of the Fund do not have to file Form 700 pursuant to section g7200.
Next, we will look at the second prong of your question: whether or not the mufual
fund managers should be filing as "consultants" under CCWD's conflict of interest code2 and
section 87300.
Section 82048 and regulation I 8701 (a) provide definitions of the term "public
official" to include a "consultant of a state or local govefirment agency . . . .,, Regulation
187 0l(a)Q) defines "consultant" as:
". . . an individual who, pursuant to a contract with a state or local
government agency:
(A) Makes a govemmental decision whether to:
1. Approve arate, rule, orregulation;
2. Adopt or enforce alaw;
3. Issue, deny, suspend, orrevoke anypermit,license, application, certificate,
approval, order, or similar authoization or entitlement;
4. Authoize the agency to enter into, modifi, or renew a contract provided it
is the type of contractthatrequires agency approval;
5. Grant agency approval to a contract that requires agency approval and to
which the agency is a party, or to the specifications for such aiontract;
6. Grant agency approval to a plan, design, report, study, or similar item;
7. Adopt, or grant agency approval of policies, standards, or guidelines for
the agency, or for any subdivision thereof; or
(B) Serves in a staff capacity with the agency and in that capacity participates
' CCWD's territorial jurisdictional boundary includes property located both in Contra Costa County
and in Alameda County. It is therefore a multi-county district falling within the provisions of section g20llia),
which means that its code reviewing body is the Commission. Because the Commission is the code reviewing'
body for CCWD, it is authorized to provide advice with regard CCWD's conflict of interest code. (Regulatio"n
18329.s.)


File No. A-06-077a
Page No. 5
in making a govemmental decision as defined in regulation 18702.2 or
performs the same or substantially allthe same duties for the agency that
would otherwise be performed by an individual holding a position specified in
the agency's Conflict of Interest Code under Government Code section
87302."
Regulation 18701(a)Q) establishes two criteria for qualification as a consultant and
an individual who satisfies either criterion is a consultant for pu{poses of the Act. First, an
individual may be a "consultant" if the individual performs, pursuant to contract, any of the
actions described in subsections (a)(2)(A)(1) - (7). Alternatively, anindividual ^uyb" 
u
consultant if the individual "serves in a staff capacity with the agency''or "performs the same
or substantially all the same duties for the agerrcy that would oth"ni." be performed by [a
staff mernberl" under subsection (a)(2)(B).
According to your facts, it does not appear thatthemutual fund employees perform
any of the duties specified in subpart (A) of subdivision (a)Q) of the regulation. They invest
CCWD's funds, along with other investors' funds, but they do not upp.urto authorize or
grant approval for CCWD to enter into any contracts. Therefore, we must determine if the
mutual fund employees meet the second criterion; i.e., do the mutual fund anployees serve in
a staff capacity? As detailed above, a person does not serve in a staff capacity unless the
person performs substantially all the same tasks as would be performed by a staff member
and engages in a govemmental decisionmaking process (either making, participating in
making or influencing the decision). Based on the information you have provi-cled, iJ does
not appear likely that the mutual fund employees perform tasks simil ar ti any staff of the
CCWD. This fact alone leads us to the conclusion that the mutual fund empioyees are not
consultants under the Act.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-
s660.
Sincerely
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
/'. 
, ./

By: Adrianne Korchmaros (,..c,-)
Political Reform Consultant II

conflict of interest code?

either as "other public officials who manage public investments" or "consultants" under the
Contra Costa Water District's conflict of interest code.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Must certain employees of Legg Mason Equity Funds file the statement of economic
interest (Form 700), whether because of section 87200 or the Contra Costa Water District's
conflict of interest code?

CONCLUSION: None of the employees of Legg Mason Equity Fund will be required to file Form 700
either as "other public officials who manage public investments" or "consultants" under the
Contra Costa Water District's conflict of interest code.
